body: Our buildkite pipeline spends 50% of its time in initializing the environment, so adding a depot should appreciably reduce CI time.
title: Add depot

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
This PR will add a public web dashboard using our Microsoft Azure server, providing an interface to select fluxnet sites to run ClimaLand simulations and generating outputs and figures demonstrating ClimaLand performance. 

## To-do
<!---  list of proposed tasks in the PR, move to "Content" on completion 
- Proposed task
-->
- [x] Make a dashboard working locally 
- [x] Host the dashboard on Azure VM
- [x] Menu for fluxnet site
- [x] Menu for figure (timeseries, diurnal, etc.)
- [x] Avoid duplicated lines of code
- [ ] Not only figure, but animation with play button (e.g., moisture and co2 profile in time)
- [ ] Add domain, timestepper, dt, etc.
- [ ] Add a few textbox to change some parameters
- [ ] "Run" button and no action on site selection. (but action on figure selection)
- [ ] Add a "download data" button (file containing inputs and outputs)
- [ ] Add a "light" and "dark" mode that adapts to the docs theme

NOTE: changing site works but is slow (~ 1 minute), because running the model is currently slow, not just on the remote but also locally.


## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
URL: [click me](https://clima.westus3.cloudapp.azure.com/jsserve/climadash)

title: Web dashboard for FLUXNET simulations O2.4.5

body:
## Purpose 
Add the option to use the TOPMODEL runoff parameterization for surface and subsurface runoff.


## To-do

## Content
1. Adds in TOPMODEL runoff parameterization. This requires adding both a new method for computing the infiltration into the soil, while accounting for surface runoff, as well as a new source term to account for subsurface runoff. 
2. Adds this option for both Richards and Energy Hydrology model
3. Adds a global simulation of Richards model with TOPMODEL runoff + PrescribedPrecipitation to buildkite
Model equations are taken from: https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdfdirect/10.1029/2005JD006111

Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.


----
- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Topmodel runoff
